data.title: INBX: Ozekibart plus FOLFIRI showed strong efficacy and safety in late-line CRC, supporting further trials
data.description:
Ozekibart plus FOLFIRI demonstrated a 20% ORR and 5.5-month median PFS in heavily pretreated CRC patients, with durable responses and a favorable safety profile. Plans are underway for first-line registrational trials and expansion into earlier lines and other tumor types.

data.title: Metropolitan Bank Holding Boosts Quarterly Dividend by 25%
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Metropolitan Bank Holding is raising its quarterly dividend by 25% to 25 cents a share, from 20 cents.

The new payout, equal to $1 a year, represents a 1.1% annual yield based on Tuesday closing price of $88.85.

The holding company of Metropolitan Commercial Bank on Tuesday reported a first-quarter profit of $31.4 million, or $2.92 a share, up from $16.4 million, or $1.45 a share, a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet were expecting a profit of $2.12 a share.

Net interest income for the quarter rose to $85.9 million from $67 million last year. The provision for credit losses fell from $4.51 million to negative $2.3 million.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Grades rise as US oil stocks fall
data.description:
Grades broadly rose on Tuesday, dealers said, after U.S. crude and fuel inventories fell.

Crude stocks fell by 4.5 million barrels in the week ended April 17, market sources said, citing American Petroleum Institute figures on Tuesday. Gasoline inventories fell by 5.2 million barrels, while distillate inventories fell by 4.6 million barrels compared to a week earlier, the sources said.

  • Light Louisiana Sweet for May delivery fell 50 cents to a midpoint of a $1.50 premium and was seen bid and offered between a $1.40 and $1.60 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures ​

  • Mars Sour rose 50 cents to a midpoint of parity and was seen bid and offered between a discount of 10 cents​​ and 10-cent a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures ​

  • WTI Midland rose 25 cents to a midpoint of a $2.75 premium and was seen bid and offered between a $2.65 and $2.85 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures ​

  • West Texas Sour rose $1.40 to a midpoint of a 25-cent discount and was seen bid and offered between a 35-cent and 15-cent a barrel discount to U.S. crude futures ​

  • WTI at East Houston, also known as MEH, traded between a $4.40 and $4.60 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures ​

  • ICE Brent June futures rose $3 to settle at $98.48 a barrel

  • WTI May crude futures rose $2.52 to settle at $92.13 a barrel

  • The Brent/WTI spread widened 44 cents to last trade at minus $8.50, after hitting a high of minus $7.61 and a low of minus $8.96

data.title: Trump Media Names McGurn Interim CEO, Succeeding Nunes
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Trump Media and Technology Group named Kevin McGurn interim chief executive officer effective immediately, succeeding Devin Nunes.

President Trump's media company did not provide a reason for the departure of Nunes, a Trump ally and former member of Congress who resigned in 2021 to helm the company.

Since December 2024, McGurn has served as an adviser to Trump Media, the company said.

McGurn will take charge of the company as it works to merge with TAE Technologies, a nuclear fusion energy company backed by Alphabet. Trump Media also operates the social media platform Truth Social, as well as the streaming service Truth+ and the financial technology brand Truth.Fi.

"Kevin brings deep experience across media, technology, and capital markets, as well as a strong understanding of Trump Media's operations and strategic priorities," Donald Trump Jr. said. "His familiarity with the Company and alignment with our leadership team uniquely position him to guide Trump Media through this important period."

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Matrix Service Company Sets Dates for Release of Third Quarter Fiscal 2026 Financial Results and Conference Call
data.description:
TULSA, Okla., April 21, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Matrix Service Company , a leading provider of engineering and construction services to the energy and industrial markets, announced today that it will release third quarter Fiscal 2026 results after market on Wednesday, May 6, 2026.

On Thursday, May 7, 2026, at 10:30 a.m. Eastern time/9:30 a.m. Central time, Matrix Service Company will host a conference call to present and discuss the Company’s financial results and forward outlook.

Earnings Conference Call instructions

Investors and other interested parties can access a live audio-visual webcast using this webcasts link, or through the Company’s website at www.matrixservicecompany.com on the Investors Relations page under Events & Presentations. Please allow extra time prior to the call to visit the site and download the streaming media software required to listen to the Internet broadcast. The conference call will be recorded and will be available for replay within one hour of completion of the live call and can be accessed following the same link as the live call.

About Matrix Service Company

Matrix Service Company is a leading specialty engineering and construction company whose commitment to safety, quality, and integrity has earned the Company a leadership position in providing infrastructure solutions across multiple end markets. Our work is foundational to helping our energy and industrial clients achieve their objectives, positively impact quality of life through the products they provide and improve the efficiency and resilience of their critical infrastructure. We pride ourselves on our commitment to our culture and core values, offering an inclusive and respectful work environment, and being certified as a Great Place To Work®.

The Company is headquartered in Tulsa, Oklahoma with offices located throughout the United States and Canada, as well as Sydney, Australia, and Seoul, South Korea. The Company reports its financial results in three key operating segments:Storage and Terminal Solutions, Utility and Power Infrastructure, and Process and Industrial Facilities.

To learn more about Matrix Service Company, visit matrixservicecompany.com

For more information about Matrix, please contact:

Investor Relations, Matrix Service Company

T:918-838-8822

Email: ir@matrixservicecompany.com

This release contains forward-looking statements that are made in reliance upon the safe harbor provisions of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. These statements are generally accompanied by words such as “anticipate,” “continues,” “expect,” “forecast,” “outlook,” “believe,” “estimate,” “should” and “will” and words of similar effect that convey future meaning, concerning the Company’s operations, economic performance and management’s best judgment as to what may occur in the future. Future events involve risks and uncertainties that may cause actual results to differ materially from those we currently anticipate. The actual results for the current and future periods and other corporate developments will depend upon a number of economic, competitive and other influences, including those factors discussed in the “Risk Factors” and “Forward Looking Statements” sections and elsewhere in the Company’s reports and filings made from time to time with the Securities and Exchange Commission. Many of these risks and uncertainties are beyond the control of the Company, and any one of which, or a combination of which, could materially and adversely affect the results of the Company's operations and its financial condition. We undertake no obligation to update information contained in this release.

data.title: Fed hawks and doves: What US central bankers are saying
data.description:
By Ann Saphir

Federal Reserve policymakers are expected to again hold short-term borrowing costs steady when they next meet April 29-30. Most Fed policymakers say a reduction in the policy rate is still a possibility but it depends on how long the Iran conflict and higher oil prices last.

Here is a look at Fed officials' recent comments, sorting them under the labels "dove" and "hawk" as shorthand for theirmonetary policy leanings. A dove is more focused on risks to the labor market and may want to cut rates more quickly, while a hawk is more focused on the threat of inflation and may be more cautious about rate cuts.

A graphic is also available.

The designations are based on public comments and published remarks. To see how Reuters' counts in each category have changed, please scroll to the bottom of this story.

Dove

Dovish

Centrist

Hawkish

Hawk

Stephen Miran, Governor, permanent voter: "I might have three (rate cuts), I might have four, I haven't made up my mind." April 16, 2026

Christopher Waller, Governor, permanent voter: "I see a forecast in which underlying inflation would continue to move toward 2%, leaving me cautious about rate cuts now and more inclined toward cuts to support the labor market later this year when the outlook is more steady." April 17, 2026

Jerome Powell, Fed Chair, permanent voter: "We feel like our policy's in a good place for us to wait and see.” March 30, 2026

Lisa Cook, Governor, permanent voter: "I would argue that the inflation risk is greater right now as a result of the Iran war." March 26, 2026

Beth Hammack, Cleveland Fed President, 2026 voter: "My baseline is that we're going to remain on hold for a good while, but I do think that there's two-sided risk to rates." April 15, 2026

Michelle Bowman, Vice Chair for Supervision, permanent voter: "I've written three cuts in for, before the end of 2026 to hopefully support the labor market." March 20, 2026

John Williams, New York Fed President, permanent voter: “Because of the energy price increases, even based on what we've seen already, inflation will be well above 3% over the next few months.” April 16, 2026

Neel Kashkari, Minneapolis Fed President, 2026 voter: No public comments on monetary policy or the economic outlook since March 3, 2026,

Lorie Logan, Dallas Fed President, 2026 voter: "I think policy is positioned to adjust to the data as it's coming in, and we're prepared to make adjustments to the policy path as appropriate." April 2, 2026

Philip Jefferson, Vice Chair, permanent voter: “I remain cautious about my outlook.... I continue, however, to see our current policy stance as appropriately positioned.” April 7, 2026

Austan Goolsbee, Chicago Fed President, 2027 voter: "I thought there could be even multiple rate cuts in 2026; the longer this goes, where we never got to see the decrease in inflation (and) if the inflation stays up, realistically, I think that starts pushing it out of '26.” April 14, 2026

Jeffrey Schmid, Kansas City Fed President, 2028 voter: "I don’t think we can be complacent about the risks to inflation expectations.” March 31, 2026

Michael Barr, Governor, permanent voter: “I am particularly concerned that yet another price shock could increase longer-term inflation expectations.” March 26, 2026

  • Thomas Barkin, Richmond Fed President, 2027 voter: "The hike case would be around inflation expectations starting to finally move
  • I don't have a sense that they've broken out at this point." March 31, 2026

Anna Paulson, Philadelphia Fed President, 2026 voter: ”There's a little bit more of a risk that the transmission of higher fuel prices, higher fertilizer prices, into inflation expectations is, you know, faster and maybe a little bit more durable….I'm worried about that.” March 27, 2026

Susan Collins, Boston Fed President, 2028 voter: No public comments on monetary policy since March 6, 2026.

Mary Daly, San Francisco Fed President, 2027 voter: "We had work to do before we had the oil price shock; with the oil price shock, the work just takes longer.” April 10, 2026

Alberto Musalem, St. Louis Fed President, 2028 voter: “I think it's probably going to be appropriate to maintain policy at this level for some time.” April 15, 2026

Notes: The current policy rate target range is 3.50%-3.75%. The median of Fed policymaker projections in March was for one quarter-percentage-point cut by the end of 2026.

The Fed's seven governors, including the central bank chief and vice chairs, are nominated by the president and confirmed by the Senate. Each of them votes at every Federal Open Market Committee meeting, held eight times a year.

Miran, Waller and Bowman are Trump nominees. Barr, Jefferson and Cook - whom Trump is attempting to fire - were nominated by former President Joe Biden. Powell was initially nominated to the Fed's Board of Governors by former President Barack Obama, elevated to the top Fed job by Trump in his first term, and renominated to that position by Biden. Trump has nominated former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh to succeed Powell when his chair term ends on May 15.

All 12 regional Fed presidents discuss and debate monetary policy at the meetings, but only five cast votes, including the New York Fed president and four others who vote for one year at a time on a rotating schedule. Fed regional bank presidents are picked by the directors of their own regional banks, subject to approval by the Fed's board.

Reuters over time has shifted policymaker designations based on fresh comments and developing circumstances. Below is a Reuters count of policymakers in each category, heading into Fed meetings.

data.title: Inhibrx Biosciences Gives Updated Data From Cancer Treatment Study
data.description:
By Christopher Kuo

Inhibrx Biosciences provided updated interim data from its Phase 1/2 study evaluating its treatment for colorectal cancer.

The biopharmaceutical company on Tuesday said its study showed an objective response rate of 20% per RECIST v1.1 criteria, meaning a fifth of the patients had a complete or partial response to the treatment.

The study evaluated the company's ozekibart treatment in combination with FOLFIRI in patients with locally advanced or metastatic, unresectable colorectal cancer. Ozekibart is an antibody treatment designed to kill cancer cells.

The company also said 42% of the trial's patients remained progression-free at the 6-month landmark.

The overall disease control rate was 87%, the company said.

The company plans to meet with the Food and Drug Administration in the second half of 2026 to discuss plans to start a first-line registrational trial. In April, the company plans to submit a Biologics License Applicataion to the FDA for ozekibart in conventional chondrosarcoma.

The FDA previously granted Fast Track designation to ozekibart in 2021.

Write to Christopher Kuo at chris.kuo@wsj.com

data.title: Pacific Sports Exchange 10-Q: Revenue $0 (Q) / $2,000 (YTD); EPS loss $(0.001) Q
data.description:
Pacific Sports Exchange reported minimal sales in its Form 10-Q, recording revenue of $0 for the three months ended February 28, 2026 and $2,000 for the six months ended February 28, 2026, with a net loss of $(11,842) for the quarter and $(21,923) year-to-date, translating to basic and diluted loss per share of $(0.001) for the quarter and $(0.00) year-to-date. The results reflect very limited revenue generation and operating losses driven primarily by professional fees and related operating expenses. Management noted organizational changes that place strategic control with a new chief executive and sole director.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 (three months ended Feb 28, 2026); $2,000 (six months ended Feb 28, 2026), reflecting minimal sales activity.
  • Gross Profit: $1,100 (three months ended Feb 28, 2026); $200 (six months ended Feb 28, 2026), a decline versus prior periods.
  • Operating Income: Loss from operations of $(11,842) (three months) and $(21,923) (six months), driven by professional fees.
  • Net Income: Net loss of $(11,842) (three months) and $(21,923) (six months), an increased loss versus prior year periods.
  • Net Income Per Share: Basic and diluted loss per common share of $(0.001) (three months) and $(0.00) (six months), reflecting a small share base.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Trend: Revenue remains minimal and largely flat, with $2.2K for the six months ended Feb 28, 2026 versus $2.0K previously, indicating limited sales traction.
  • Sales Channel Focus: Operates primarily as an online-only retailer, relying on eBay as its main marketplace while working to optimize its company website.
  • Product & Sourcing Strategy: Resells new and used tennis and golf equipment and golf carts sourced from retailers in Florida and California, trade shows and consignment arrangements.
  • Operational Shift: Expanded into consignment sales and increased inventory of used golf carts to diversify product mix and margin opportunities.
  • Organizational Change: A change in control resulted in appointment of a new CEO and sole director, enabling unilateral strategic direction and execution.

Original SEC Filing: Pacific Sports Exchange Inc. [ PSPX ] - 10-Q - Apr. 21, 2026

data.title: Prudential Financial Stock Is Falling on Report of Potential Misconduct at Japan Insurer — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Janet H. Cho

Prudential Financial Inc. stock is falling on Tuesday after a report about potential employee misconduct at Japan-based Gibraltar Life Insurance.

Japan's Nikkei.com news site reported Tuesday that "dozens of cases of suspected financial fraud by former employees of Gibraltar Life Insurance, a subsidiary of the Prudential Group have surfaced."

To "prevent recurrence," Prudential Life Insurance is considering extending the period during which it will suspend new contracts, which was set to end on May 9, by about six months, the report said, in an English language translation.

The report also said "Prudential Holdings Japan, the parent company, will make an announcement soon" as external experts investigate the extent of the damage.

Prudential Financial could not immediately be reached.

Shares of Prudential Financial were down 6% on Tuesday.

The Asahi Shimbun newspaper has reported that 107 current and former Prudential Life Insurance employees "obtained money through fraudulent means or failed to repay funds they had borrowed from roughly 500 customers," amounting to an estimated 3.14 billion yen ($20 million) between 1991 and 2025, citing the company's internal investigation released on Jan. 16.

A separate investigation found that "69 employees violated internal rules by taking kickbacks for referring customers to outside investment firms," The Asahi Shimbun reported.

In early February, Prudential announced a voluntary 90-day suspension of new sales activity to support Prudential of Japan's implementation of comprehensive operational, organizational, and governance changes to address the previously disclosed incidents of misconduct by employees.

On April 9, the newspaper said that Japan's Financial Services Agency was expanding its investigation and conducting an on-site inspection of the parent company of Prudential Life Insurance Co. and examining whether "an excessive performance-driven culture that fostered misconduct and lax oversight of sales employees" contributed to what happened.

Prudential shares had traded as low as $95.74 on Tuesday afternoon, down 6.7% and on pace for its largest percentage decrease since April 4, 2025, when shares fell 7.9%, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

Shares closed down 6% at $96.45 on Tuesday. Prudential's stock is down 14.6% this year and down 3.8% over the past 12 months.

Write to Janet H. Cho at janet.cho@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Boeing Earnings Are Coming. Can They Avoid the Aerospace 'Bloodbath?' — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Al Root

Boeing reports quarterly results on Wednesday. Progress, not perfection, is the goal.

For the quarter, Wall Street projects a per share loss of 68 cents from sales of $21.3 billion, according to FactSet. A year ago, Boeing reported a per share loss of 49 cents from sales of $19.5 billion.

Sales are up on higher jet deliveries. Boeing delivered 143 commercial planes in the first three months of the year, up from 130 a year ago.

Growth is good. Still, "it's been a slow start to the year for Airbus and Boeing, with both OEMs being impacted by manufacturing issues (A320 fuselage panels, 737 wiring)," wrote Vertical Research Partners analyst Rob Stallard in a preview report. The underlying supply chain, however, should be OK with steady progress allowing the plane makers to boost 2026 production.

Boeing is expected to deliver about 660 planes in 2026, up from 600 in 2025.

Boeing is a turnaround story, which might insulate it from other aerospace concerns, such as the problem of high oil prices, which can impact demand for air travel.

Tuesday wasn't pretty for aerospace and defense companies. Shares of GE Aerospace, RTX, and Northrop Grumman fell about 6%, 4%, and 7%, respectively, on Tuesday. All three reported better-than-expected first quarter numbers.

It was a "bloodbath," said Stallard. "While it may seem pretty obvious, we think investors have been reluctant to acknowledge that the War in the Middle East is more negative for the aerospace sector than understood. We're not talking a COVID style Aero-mageddon, but in our estimate a continuation of Middle Eastern flight schedule reductions could take about 3% off global [traffic] growth this year."

The war could be reflected in Boeing's commentary about deliveries, which could be back-end loaded.

Boeing still has some internal issues that can move shares, such as approvals of new jet versions.

"We view certification of the -7 [version] and especially the -10 [version of the] MAX variant as very important for Boeing's margin profile in 2027," wrote RBC analyst Ken Herbert in a preview report. "We believe the -10 carries a strong pricing profile and will likely be critical for margins inflecting positive in 2027."

Updates on plane certifications by aviation authorities are another thing for investors to watch for.

Coming into the week, shares were down roughly 10% since the first quarter earnings report and down about 2% since the fighting started in Iran.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Society Pass Received Notice of Noncompliance
data.description:
On April 16, 2026, the company received a Nasdaq notice that it is not in compliance with Listing Rule 5250(c)(1) for failing to timely file its Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025. The notice has no immediate effect; the company has 60 days to submit a plan (by June 15, 2026) and, if accepted, may have until October 12, 2026 to regain compliance.

data.title: NETSTREIT launches $400M at-the-market equity program, terminates prior $300M ATM
data.description:
NETSTREIT launched a new at-the-market equity program to sell up to $400 million of common stock and terminated its prior ATM agreement.

Key Highlights:

  • Entered a sales agreement with multiple agents to offer up to $400 million of common stock through an at-the-market program.
  • Agents’ commissions capped at 1.5%; forward-sale related commissions also capped at 1.5% of gross sales price.
  • Proceeds intended to be contributed to the operating partnership for acquisitions, development, investments, working capital and debt repayment.
  • Terminated prior ATM; had sold roughly $256.1 million under the prior agreement and has 12,777,902 forward-settlement shares maturing Sept 2026–Mar 2027.

Original SEC Filing: NETSTREIT Corp. [ NTST ] - 8-K - Apr. 21, 2026

data.title: Journey Medical expands Emrosi payer access to 85% of US commercial lives
data.description:
Journey Medical announced Emrosi payer access now reaches ~85% of U.S. commercial lives following a third major GPO contract.

Key Highlights:

  • Contract with a third major GPO expands Emrosi access from ~100M to over 150M commercial lives as of April 1, 2026.
  • Approximately 85% of all U.S. commercial lives now have access to Emrosi for inflammatory lesions of rosacea in adults.
  • Expanded payer access is expected to facilitate growth in prescription demand and support continued launch execution.
  • Company aims to convert access into formulary coverage, reducing reliance on co-pay bridging and improving brand profitability.

Original SEC Filing: Journey Medical Corp [ DERM ] - 8-K - Apr. 21, 2026

data.description:
Independent Bank reported 2025 net income of $68.5 million and announced a $1.04 per share cash dividend at its 2026 annual meeting presentation.

Key Highlights:

  • Reported 2025 net income of $68.5M and diluted EPS of $3.27, reflecting continued profitability.
  • Paid $1.04 per share in cash dividends for 2025, an 8% increase year-over-year.
  • Loan growth of $237.5M (5.9%); commercial loans grew $276.2M (14.3%); loan-to-deposit ratio 89.8%.
  • Tangible book value per share rose $2.71 (13.4%) to $23.04; returned 31.5% of 2025 earnings via dividends.
  • Asset quality remained strong with reserves/loans at 1.48% and NPAs/assets of 0.44% for 2025.

data.title: Interactive Brokers' quarterly profit rises on robust trading activity
data.description:
Trading platform Interactive Brokers reported a 25% jump in first-quarter profit on Tuesday, helped by higher customer trading volumes.

The quarter was marked by market volatility, driven by concerns over AI disrupting business models and an escalating conflict in the Middle East that rattled oil markets.

Volatility is often a boon for trading desks, as investors rejig portfolios to hedge risks.

Here are some details:

  • Commission revenue surged 19% to $613 million compared to the previous year, driven by a strong jump in customer stock, futures and options trading volumes in the three months ended March 31.

  • Net interest income rose 17% to $904 million for the quarter, owing to higher average customer margin loans and credit balances.

  • The Connecticut-based company allows customers to buy and sell stocks, options, futures, cryptocurrencies, bonds, mutual funds, ETFs and precious metals.

  • Daily average revenue trades, or DARTs, which represent the average trades per day generating commissions or fees, rose 24% in the quarter to 4.37 million trades.

  • The company reported net income available for common stockholders of $267 million or 59 cents per share for the first quarter, compared with $213 million or 48 cents per share in the previous year.
